sn46.55:1.2.pli: Atha kho saṅgāravo brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
sn46.55:1.2.eng: Then Saṅgārava the brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.

sn46.55:1.3.pli: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho saṅgāravo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn46.55:1.3.eng: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:

sn46.55:2.1.pli: “Ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo yenekadā dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā?
sn46.55:2.1.eng: “What is the cause, Mister Gotama, what is the reason why sometimes even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed?

sn46.55:2.2.pli: Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo yenekadā dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā”ti?
sn46.55:2.2.eng: And why is it that sometimes even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed?”

sn46.55:3.1.pli: “Yasmiṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, samaye kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:3.1.eng: “Brahmin, there’s a time when your heart is overcome and mired in sensual desire and you don’t truly understand the escape from sensual desire that has arisen. At that time you don’t truly know or see what is good for yourself, good for another, or good for both. Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:4.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto saṁsaṭṭho lākhāya vā haliddiyā vā nīliyā vā mañjiṭṭhāya vā.
sn46.55:4.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water that was mixed with dye such as red lac, turmeric, indigo, or rose madder.

sn46.55:4.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya.
sn46.55:4.2.eng: Even a person with clear eyes checking their own reflection wouldn’t truly know it or see it.

sn46.55:4.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati …pe… paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:4.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is overcome and mired in sensual desire … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:5.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye byāpādapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati byāpādaparetena, uppannassa ca byāpādassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:5.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is overcome and mired in ill will … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:6.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto agginā santatto pakkuthito usmudakajāto.
sn46.55:6.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water that was heated by fire, boiling and bubbling.

sn46.55:6.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya.
sn46.55:6.2.eng: Even a person with clear eyes checking their own reflection wouldn’t truly know it or see it.

sn46.55:6.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye byāpādapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati byāpādaparetena, uppannassa ca byāpādassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi tasmiṁ samaye …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:6.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is overcome and mired in ill will … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:7.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye thinamiddhapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati thinamiddhaparetena, uppannassa ca thinamiddhassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:7.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is overcome and mired in dullness and drowsiness … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:8.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water overgrown with moss and aquatic plants.

sn46.55:8.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya.
sn46.55:8.2.eng: Even a person with clear eyes checking their own reflection wouldn’t truly know it or see it.

sn46.55:8.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye thinamiddhapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati thinamiddhaparetena, uppannassa ca thinamiddhassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:8.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is overcome and mired in dullness and drowsiness … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:9.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye uddhaccakukkuccapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati uddhaccakukkuccaparetena, uppannassa ca uddhaccakukkuccassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:9.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is overcome and mired in restlessness and remorse … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:10.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto vāterito calito bhanto ūmijāto.
sn46.55:10.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water stirred by the wind, churning, swirling, and rippling.

sn46.55:10.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya.
sn46.55:10.2.eng: Even a person with clear eyes checking their own reflection wouldn’t truly know it or see it.

sn46.55:10.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye uddhaccakukkuccapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati uddhaccakukkuccaparetena, uppannassa ca uddhaccakukkuccassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:10.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is overcome and mired in restlessness and remorse … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:11.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi … dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:11.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is overcome and mired in doubt … Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:12.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto āvilo luḷito kalalībhūto andhakāre nikkhitto.
sn46.55:12.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water that was cloudy, murky, and muddy, hidden in the darkness.

sn46.55:12.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya.
sn46.55:12.2.eng: Even a person with clear eyes checking their own reflection wouldn’t truly know it or see it.

sn46.55:12.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, paratthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati, ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati; dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:12.3.eng: In the same way, there’s a time when your heart is overcome and mired in doubt and you don’t truly understand the escape from doubt that has arisen. At that time you don’t truly know or see what is good for yourself, good for another, or good for both. Even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:12.4.pli: Ayaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yenekadā dīgharattaṁ sajjhāyakatāpi mantā nappaṭibhanti, pageva asajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:12.4.eng: This is the cause, brahmin, this is the reason why sometimes even hymns that are long-rehearsed don’t spring to mind, let alone those that are not rehearsed.

sn46.55:13.1.pli: Yasmiñca kho, brāhmaṇa, samaye na kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati; dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:13.1.eng: There’s a time when your heart is not overcome and mired in sensual desire and you truly understand the escape from sensual desire that has arisen. At that time you truly know and see what is good for yourself, good for another, and good for both. Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:14.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto asaṁsaṭṭho lākhāya vā haliddiyā vā nīliyā vā mañjiṭṭhāya vā.
sn46.55:14.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water that was not mixed with dye such as red lac, turmeric, indigo, or rose madder.

sn46.55:14.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya.
sn46.55:14.2.eng: A person with clear eyes checking their own reflection would truly know it and see it.

sn46.55:14.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe….
sn46.55:14.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is not overcome and mired in sensual desire … Even hymns that are long-unpracticed spring to mind, let alone those that are practiced.

sn46.55:15.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na byāpādapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na byāpādaparetena, uppannassa ca byāpādassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi …pe… dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:15.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is not overcome and mired in ill will … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:16.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto na agginā santatto na pakkuthito na usmudakajāto.
sn46.55:16.1.eng: Suppose there is a bowl of water that is not heated by a fire, boiling and bubbling.

sn46.55:16.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya.
sn46.55:16.2.eng: A person with clear eyes checking their own reflection would truly know it and see it.

sn46.55:16.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na byāpādapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na byāpādaparetena, uppannassa ca byāpādassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi …pe… dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:16.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is not overcome and mired in ill will … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:17.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na thinamiddhapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na thinamiddhaparetena, uppannassa ca thinamiddhassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi …pe… dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:17.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is not overcome and mired in dullness and drowsiness … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:18.1.eng: Suppose there is a bowl of water that is not overgrown with moss and aquatic plants.

sn46.55:18.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya.
sn46.55:18.2.eng: A person with clear eyes checking their own reflection would truly know it and see it.

sn46.55:18.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na thinamiddhapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na thinamiddhaparetena, uppannassa ca thinamiddhassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi …pe… dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:18.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is not overcome and mired in dullness and drowsiness … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:19.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na uddhaccakukkuccapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na uddhaccakukkuccaparetena, uppannassa ca uddhaccakukkuccassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi …pe… dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:19.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is not overcome and mired in restlessness and remorse … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:20.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto na vāterito na calito na bhanto na ūmijāto.
sn46.55:20.1.eng: Suppose there is a bowl of water that is not stirred by the wind, churning, swirling, and rippling.

sn46.55:20.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya.
sn46.55:20.2.eng: A person with clear eyes checking their own reflection would truly know it and see it.

sn46.55:20.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na uddhaccakukkuccapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na uddhaccakukkuccaparetena, uppannassa ca uddhaccakukkuccassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi …pe… ubhayatthampi …pe… dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:20.3.eng: In the same way, when your heart is not overcome and mired in restlessness and remorse … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:21.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati; ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati; dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:21.1.eng: Furthermore, when your heart is not overcome and mired in doubt … Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:22.1.pli: Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, udapatto accho vippasanno anāvilo āloke nikkhitto.
sn46.55:22.1.eng: Suppose there was a bowl of water that was transparent, clear, and unclouded, brought into the light.

sn46.55:22.2.pli: Tattha cakkhumā puriso sakaṁ mukhanimittaṁ paccavekkhamāno yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya.
sn46.55:22.2.eng: A person with clear eyes checking their own reflection would truly know it and see it.

sn46.55:22.3.pli: Evameva kho, brāhmaṇa, yasmiṁ samaye na vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati na vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, attatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, paratthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati, ubhayatthampi tasmiṁ samaye yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati; dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:22.3.eng: In the same way, there’s a time when your heart is not overcome and mired in doubt and you truly understand the escape from doubt that has arisen. At that time you truly know and see what is good for yourself, good for another, and good for both. Even hymns that are long-unrehearsed spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:22.4.pli: Ayaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yenekadā dīgharattaṁ asajjhāyakatāpi mantā paṭibhanti, pageva sajjhāyakatā.
sn46.55:22.4.eng: This is the cause, brahmin, this is the reason why sometimes even hymns that are long-unrehearsed do spring to mind, let alone those that are rehearsed.

sn46.55:23.1.pli: Sattime, brāhmaṇa, bojjhaṅgā anāvaraṇā anīvaraṇā cetaso anupakkilesā bhāvitā bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiphalasacchikiriyāya saṁvattanti.
sn46.55:23.1.eng: These seven awakening factors are not obstacles, hindrances, or corruptions of the mind. When developed and cultivated they lead to the realization of the fruit of knowledge and freedom.

sn46.55:23.3.pli: Satisambojjhaṅgo kho, brāhmaṇa, anāvaraṇo anīvaraṇo cetaso anupakkileso bhāvito bahulīkato vijjāvimuttiphalasacchikiriyāya saṁvattati …pe… upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo kho, brāhmaṇa, anāvaraṇo anīvaraṇo cetaso anupakkileso bhāvito bahulīkato vijjāvimuttiphalasacchikiriyāya saṁvattati.
sn46.55:23.3.eng: The awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.

sn46.55:23.4.pli: Ime kho, brāhmaṇa, satta bojjhaṅgā anāvaraṇā anīvaraṇā cetaso anupakkilesā bhāvitā bahulīkatā vijjāvimuttiphalasacchikiriyāya saṁvattantī”ti.
sn46.55:23.4.eng: These seven awakening factors are not obstacles, hindrances, or corruptions of the mind. When developed and cultivated they lead to the realization of the fruit of knowledge and freedom.”

sn46.55:24.1.pli: Evaṁ vutte, saṅgāravo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn46.55:24.1.eng: When he said this, Saṅgārava said to the Buddha,

sn46.55:24.3.pli: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.
sn46.55:24.3.eng: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

sn46.56:1.2.pli: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
sn46.56:1.2.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.

sn46.56:1.3.pli: Atha kho abhayo rājakumāro yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho abhayo rājakumāro bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
sn46.56:1.3.eng: Then Prince Abhaya went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

sn46.56:1.5.eng: ‘There is no cause or reason for not knowing and not seeing.

sn46.56:1.6.eng: Not knowing and not seeing have no cause or reason.

sn46.56:1.7.pli: Natthi hetu, natthi paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
sn46.56:1.7.eng: There is no cause or reason for knowing and seeing.

sn46.56:1.8.eng: Knowing and seeing have no cause or reason.’

sn46.56:1.9.eng: What does the Buddha say about this?”

sn46.56:1.10.pli: “Atthi, rājakumāra, hetu, atthi paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya.
sn46.56:1.10.eng: “Prince, there are causes and reasons for not knowing and not seeing.

sn46.56:1.11.eng: Not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons.

sn46.56:1.12.pli: Atthi, rājakumāra, hetu, atthi paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
sn46.56:1.12.eng: There are causes and reasons for knowing and seeing.

sn46.56:1.13.eng: Knowing and seeing have causes and reasons.”

sn46.56:2.1.pli: “Katamo pana, bhante, hetu, katamo paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya?
sn46.56:2.1.eng: “But sir, what is the cause and reason for not knowing and not seeing?

sn46.56:2.2.pli: Kathaṁ sahetu, sappaccayo aññāṇaṁ adassanaṁ hotī”ti?
sn46.56:2.2.eng: How do not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons?”

sn46.56:2.3.pli: “Yasmiṁ kho, rājakumāra, samaye kāmarāgapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati kāmarāgaparetena, uppannassa ca kāmarāgassa nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati—
sn46.56:2.3.eng: “There’s a time when the heart is overcome and mired in sensual desire, without truly knowing and seeing the escape from sensual desire that has arisen.

sn46.56:2.4.pli: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya.
sn46.56:2.4.eng: This is a cause and reason for not knowing and not seeing.

sn46.56:2.5.pli: Evampi sahetu sappaccayo aññāṇaṁ adassanaṁ hoti.
sn46.56:2.5.eng: And this is how not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons.

sn46.56:3.1.pli: Puna caparaṁ, rājakumāra, yasmiṁ samaye byāpādapariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati byāpādaparetena …pe…
sn46.56:3.1.eng: Furthermore, there’s a time when the heart is overcome and mired in ill will …

sn46.56:3.4.pli: vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhitena cetasā viharati vicikicchāparetena, uppannāya ca vicikicchāya nissaraṇaṁ yathābhūtaṁ na jānāti na passati—
sn46.56:3.4.eng: doubt, without truly knowing and seeing the escape from doubt that has arisen.

sn46.56:3.5.pli: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo aññāṇāya adassanāya.
sn46.56:3.5.eng: This is a cause and reason for not knowing and not seeing.

sn46.56:3.6.pli: Evampi sahetu sappaccayo aññāṇaṁ adassanaṁ hotī”ti.
sn46.56:3.6.eng: And this is how not knowing and not seeing have causes and reasons.”

sn46.56:4.1.eng: “Sir, what is the name of this exposition of the teaching?”

sn46.56:4.4.eng: Indeed, Holy One, these are hindrances!

sn46.56:4.5.pli: Ekamekenapi kho, bhante, nīvaraṇena abhibhūto yathābhūtaṁ na jāneyya na passeyya, ko pana vādo pañcahi nīvaraṇehi?
sn46.56:4.5.eng: Overcome by even a single hindrance you wouldn’t truly know or see, let alone all five hindrances.

sn46.56:5.1.pli: Katamo pana, bhante, hetu, katamo paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya?
sn46.56:5.1.eng: But sir, what is the cause and reason for knowing and seeing?

sn46.56:5.2.pli: Kathaṁ sahetu, sappaccayo ñāṇaṁ dassanaṁ hotī”ti?
sn46.56:5.2.eng: How do knowing and seeing have causes and reasons?”

sn46.56:5.3.pli: “Idha, rājakumāra, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn46.56:5.3.eng: “It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factor of mindfulness, which relies on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripens as letting go.

sn46.56:5.4.pli: So satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāvitena cittena yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati—
sn46.56:5.4.eng: They truly know and see with a mind that has developed the awakening factor of mindfulness.

sn46.56:5.5.pli: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
sn46.56:5.5.eng: This is a cause and reason for knowing and seeing.

sn46.56:5.6.pli: Evampi sahetu, sappaccayo ñāṇaṁ dassanaṁ hoti.
sn46.56:5.6.eng: And this is how knowing and seeing have causes and reasons.

sn46.56:6.1.eng: Furthermore, a mendicant develops the awakening factor of investigation of principles … energy … rapture … tranquility … immersion …

sn46.56:6.2.pli: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn46.56:6.2.eng: equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn46.56:6.3.pli: So upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāvitena cittena yathābhūtaṁ jānāti passati—
sn46.56:6.3.eng: They truly know and see with a mind that has developed the awakening factor of equanimity.

sn46.56:6.4.pli: ayampi kho, rājakumāra, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo ñāṇāya dassanāya.
sn46.56:6.4.eng: This is a cause and reason for knowing and seeing.

sn46.56:6.5.pli: Evaṁ sahetu, sappaccayo ñāṇaṁ dassanaṁ hotī”ti.
sn46.56:6.5.eng: And this is how knowing and seeing have causes and reasons.”

sn46.56:7.1.eng: “Sir, what is the name of this exposition of the teaching?”

sn46.56:7.3.eng: “Indeed, Blessed One, these are awakening factors!

sn46.56:7.4.eng: Indeed, Holy One, these are awakening factors!

sn46.56:7.5.pli: Ekamekenapi kho, bhante, bojjhaṅgena samannāgato yathābhūtaṁ jāneyya passeyya, ko pana vādo sattahi bojjhaṅgehi?
sn46.56:7.5.eng: Endowed with even a single awakening factor you would truly know and see, let alone all seven awakening factors.

sn46.56:7.6.pli: Yopi me, bhante, gijjhakūṭaṁ pabbataṁ ārohantassa kāyakilamatho cittakilamatho, sopi me paṭippassaddho, dhammo ca me abhisamito”ti.
sn46.56:7.6.eng: When climbing Mount Vulture’s Peak I became fatigued in body and mind. But this has now faded away. And I’ve comprehended the teaching.”

sn46.57:1.2.pli: “Aṭṭhikasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
sn46.57:1.2.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a skeleton is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.

sn46.57:1.3.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?

sn46.57:1.4.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
sn46.57:1.4.eng: It’s when a mendicant develops the perception of a skeleton together with the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,

sn46.57:1.5.pli: aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
sn46.57:1.5.eng: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.

sn46.57:1.6.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā”ti.
sn46.57:1.6.eng: That’s how the perception of a skeleton, when developed and cultivated, is very fruitful and beneficial.”

sn46.57:2.1.pli: “Aṭṭhikasaññāya, bhikkhave, bhāvitāya bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā.
sn46.57:2.1.eng: “When the perception of a skeleton is developed and cultivated you can expect one of two results: enlightenment in this very life, or if there’s something left over, non-return.

sn46.57:2.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitāya ca kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññāya kathaṁ bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā?

sn46.57:2.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…

sn46.57:2.4.pli: aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn46.57:2.5.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññāya evaṁ bahulīkatāya dvinnaṁ phalānaṁ aññataraṁ phalaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—diṭṭheva dhamme aññā, sati vā upādisese anāgāmitā”ti.

sn46.57:3.1.pli: “Aṭṭhikasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahato atthāya saṁvattati.
sn46.57:3.1.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a skeleton is developed and cultivated it leads to great benefit.

sn46.57:3.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahato atthāya saṁvattati?

sn46.57:3.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…

sn46.57:3.4.pli: aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn46.57:3.5.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahato atthāya saṁvattatī”ti.

sn46.57:4.1.pli: “Aṭṭhikasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahato yogakkhemāya saṁvattati.
sn46.57:4.1.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a skeleton is developed and cultivated it leads to great sanctuary from the yoke.

sn46.57:4.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahato yogakkhemāya saṁvattati?

sn46.57:4.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…

sn46.57:4.4.pli: aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn46.57:4.5.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahato yogakkhemāya saṁvattatī”ti.

sn46.57:5.1.pli: “Aṭṭhikasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahato saṁvegāya saṁvattati.
sn46.57:5.1.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a skeleton is developed and cultivated it leads to great inspiration.

sn46.57:5.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahato saṁvegāya saṁvattati?

sn46.57:5.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…

sn46.57:5.4.pli: aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn46.57:5.5.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahato saṁvegāya saṁvattatī”ti.

sn46.57:6.1.pli: “Aṭṭhikasaññā, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahato phāsuvihārāya saṁvattati.
sn46.57:6.1.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a skeleton is developed and cultivated it leads to dwelling in great ease.

sn46.57:6.2.pli: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahato phāsuvihārāya saṁvattati?

sn46.57:6.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti …pe…

sn46.57:6.4.pli: aṭṭhikasaññāsahagataṁ upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.

sn46.57:6.5.pli: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhikasaññā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahato phāsuvihārāya saṁvattatī”ti

sn46.58:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a worm-infested corpse is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial. …”

sn46.59:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, when the perception of a livid corpse is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial. …”
